pe:description: Four-phase manual pipeline: hand-author JSONLD app skeleton defining spatial zones, navigation layers, nav items, and zone components; validate against DS-ONT business rules BR-DS-013/014/015; test with runtime loader (app-skeleton-loader.js); commit and verify deployment. Skeletons are structural — they define WHERE UI elements live, not HOW they look.
pe:businessObjective: Produce machine-readable app skeleton JSONLD that the runtime loader can parse into state.zoneRegistry and state.navLayerRegistry, enabling data-driven UI construction via the 4-tier EMC cascade (PFC → PFI → Product → App).
pe:scope: One skeleton JSONLD file per cascade tier per application. PFC base skeleton is authored once; PFI/Product/App overrides extend it. Output is committed to DS-ONT instance-data/ and deployed via GitHub Pages.

pe:inputs.pe:name: Cascade Tier
pe:inputs.pe:description: Which cascade tier this skeleton belongs to: PFC (base), PFI (instance override), Product, or App

pe:inputs.pe:name: Application ID
pe:inputs.pe:description: Unique application identifier for the skeleton (e.g. pfc-visualiser, baiv-dashboard)

pe:inputs.pe:name: Base Skeleton File
pe:inputs.pe:description: Path to the PFC base skeleton that a PFI/Product/App override extends. Required for non-PFC tiers.

pe:hasPhase.pe:description: Hand-author the app skeleton JSONLD file with Application, AppZone, NavLayer, NavItem, and ZoneComponent entities following DS-ONT v2.0.0 schema.
pe:hasPhase.pe:entryConditions: DS-ONT v2.0.0 schema available; PE-DS-EXTRACT-002-guide.md entity reference consulted; zone/nav design finalised
pe:hasPhase.pe:exitConditions: Valid JSONLD file with @context, @graph containing typed entities; all required properties populated; enum values valid
pe:activities: 1. Create JSONLD file with @context: { ds: 'https://platformcore.io/ontology/ds/' }.
2. Add ds:Application root entity with appId, appName, version, appType, cascadeTier.
3. Add ds:AppZone entries for each spatial zone with zoneId, zoneName, zoneType, defaultVisible, cascadeTier.
4. Add ds:NavLayer entries (L1-L4) with layerId, layerName, layerLevel, renderOrder, cascadeTier.
5. Add ds:NavItem entries for each nav action with itemId, label, itemType, action, renderOrder, cascadeTier, ds:belongsToLayer reference.
6. Add ds:ZoneComponent entries binding DesignComponents to zones with placementId, renderOrder, cascadeTier, ds:placedInZone and ds:placesComponent references.
7. For PFI overrides: only add new entities or entities that extend/hide PFC items — do not duplicate the full PFC base.

pe:hasPhase.pe:description: Validate the authored skeleton against DS-ONT business rules BR-DS-013 (CascadeTierImmutability), BR-DS-014 (ZonesMustHaveType), and BR-DS-015 (NavItemMustHaveAction).
pe:hasPhase.pe:entryConditions: JSONLD file authored in Phase 1; DS-ONT schema loaded for reference
pe:hasPhase.pe:exitConditions: Zero severity=error violations; all AppZones have zoneType+defaultVisible; all non-Separator NavItems have action
pe:activities: 1. BR-DS-013: Verify PFI/Product/App overrides do not modify structural properties (zoneType, position, layerLevel, action) of PFC-tier entities.
2. BR-DS-014: Verify every AppZone has zoneType and defaultVisible set.
3. BR-DS-015: Verify every NavItem (except Separator) has a non-empty action string.
4. Check all @id values are unique within the file and across base+override when merging.
5. Verify all ds:placedInZone references resolve to valid zone @id values.
6. Verify all ds:belongsToLayer references resolve to valid NavLayer @id values.

pe:hasPhase.pe:description: Verify the runtime loader (app-skeleton-loader.js) parses the skeleton correctly and populates state.zoneRegistry and state.navLayerRegistry.
pe:hasPhase.pe:entryConditions: GATE-2 passed; app-skeleton-loader.js available; vitest configured
pe:hasPhase.pe:exitConditions: All existing tests pass; loader parses skeleton without errors; registries populated with correct counts
pe:activities: 1. Run existing loader tests: npx vitest run tests/app-skeleton-loader.test.js.
2. Run design-token-tree tests: npx vitest run tests/design-token-tree.test.js.
3. If adding new zones/items, add corresponding test cases to verify parsing.
4. Manual browser test: open visualiser, verify admin panel shows skeleton data with cascade tier badges.
5. For PFI overrides: test mergeSkeletonCascade(base, override) produces correct merged output.

pe:hasPhase.pe:description: Stage the skeleton JSONLD, commit with standard message format, push, and verify GitHub Pages deployment.
pe:hasPhase.pe:entryConditions: GATE-3 passed; all tests green; skeleton file on disk
pe:hasPhase.pe:exitConditions: git push succeeds; GitHub Actions Pages workflow completed; JSONLD accessible on GitHub Pages
pe:activities: 1. Run full test suite: npx vitest run from visualiser dir.
2. Stage skeleton file: git add instance-data/{name}-app-skeleton-*.jsonld.
3. Commit: feat(ds): add {NAME} app skeleton via PE-DS-EXTRACT-002.
4. Pull and rebase: git pull --rebase.
5. Push: git push.
6. Verify: gh run list --limit 1; confirm workflow completed.
7. Verify JSONLD accessible on GitHub Pages URL.

pe:errorRecovery.rebaseFailed: Stash local changes (git stash), pull --rebase, pop stash, resolve conflicts if any, then re-commit and push.
pe:errorRecovery.pushRejected: Pull --rebase first, then push again. Never force-push to main.
pe:errorRecovery.testsFailed: Abort commit. Investigate and fix the failing test before retrying.

pe:hasGate.pe:description: JSONLD file is valid JSON with correct @context, all required properties present, enum values valid, @id values unique
pe:hasGate.pe:criteria: Valid JSON; all required properties per entity type present; all enum values from DS-ONT controlled vocabulary; no duplicate @id

pe:hasGate.pe:description: Zero blocking errors from BR-DS-013 (CascadeTierImmutability), BR-DS-014 (ZonesMustHaveType), BR-DS-015 (NavItemMustHaveAction)
pe:hasGate.pe:criteria: Zero severity=error rule violations; all reference @id values resolve

pe:hasGate.pe:description: All vitest tests pass; app-skeleton-loader.js parses skeleton without errors; state.zoneRegistry and state.navLayerRegistry populated with correct counts
pe:hasGate.pe:criteria: vitest exit code 0; state.zoneRegistry.size matches expected zone count; state.navLayerRegistry.size matches expected layer count

pe:hasGate.pe:description: Push succeeded; GitHub Pages workflow completed; JSONLD accessible at deployed URL
pe:hasGate.pe:criteria: git push exit code 0; gh run list shows status=completed/success; HTTP 200 from GitHub Pages URL with valid JSON response

pe:augmentedBy.pe:description: Claude Code agent assisting with skeleton JSONLD authoring, validation against DS-ONT business rules, and commit/deploy workflow
pe:augmentedBy.pe:capabilities: JSONLD generation; DS-ONT schema validation (BR-DS-013/014/015); vitest execution; git operations; GitHub Pages verification

pe:relatedProcesses.pe:description: Automated pipeline extracting design tokens from Figma into DS-ONT instance data. Separate concern — tokens are visual (colours, spacing), skeletons are structural (zones, navigation).

pe:versionHistory.pe:changes: Initial 4-phase pipeline: Author JSONLD, Validate Rules, Test with Loader, Commit & Verify. 4 gates, 1 agent, 2 metrics. Covers all 5 skeleton entity types (Application, AppZone, NavLayer, NavItem, ZoneComponent) and 3 business rules (BR-DS-013/014/015). Cross-references PE-DS-EXTRACT-001 as complementary process.
